concerns.text: Phase 6 tasks (T039-T042) depend on 'US2 analysis logic' and 'feedback rules' (per 'Dependencies' section), but T039 is tagged [US3]. This creates a semantic ordering violation: a task in US3 (Phase 5) cannot depend on logic from US2 (Phase 4) if US3 is intended to be independently shippable. The 'Dependencies' section explicitly states Phase 6 depends on US2, yet the task list places these tasks under the US3 header, breaking the 'User-story groups' independence rule.

Task T011 (US1) implements 'generate synthetic data' logic. However, T010 (Contract test for data schema) is listed as a prerequisite test for US1. If T010 validates against a schema that *requires* real data (as per spec FR-002), T010 will fail before T011 can generate the synthetic fallback. The order implies T010 runs first, but the logic flow (try real -> fail -> generate synthetic) requires the generation logic to be available to the test runner or the test to be conditional. The current ordering suggests a rigid sequence that may not support the 'fail gracefully' flow described in the spec.

Phase 6 is labeled 'Longitudinal Drift & Feedback Loop Analysis (Priority: P3 - Review Revision)' but the tasks T039-T042 are tagged [US3]. This is a labeling inconsistency. If this is a new user story (US4 or US6), it should have its own phase header and ID. If it is part of US3, the dependency on US2 logic (as stated in the 'Dependencies' section) violates the 'independently-shippable unit' rule for US3.
